hook: IRNA English and Foreign Affairs — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Strait of Hormuz / US-Iran deal. Nobody else did.

The paradox: Iranian state propaganda and the flagship journal of the US foreign-policy establishment independently reach the same conclusion: two months of American military pressure has failed to achieve its objectives, and the US will be the war's strategic loser. (IRNA Engli…

Silence as data: `south_korea` didn't cover this — they covered Yonhap ran two items: domestic fuel price caps and a request for North Korean women's football team visit — Hormuz diplomacy entirely absent despite South Korea's heavy LNG dependence. instead.

**Folha de São Paulo** (brazil): Reports that Saudi Arabia denied airspace rights to the US after Trump announced naval escort convoys through Hormuz, frustrating the plan — a consequential diplomatic fact not prominently covered in Anglophone press.
